Maps Make your way to (and around) Port A Whether you're driving in from the south side of Mustang Island or across the ferry from the north, you're never too far from Port A. Even though it's a short drive from home, your worries will feel like they're a world away once you take that first breath of salty sea air. Wherever you stay in Port A, you'll have direct access to the beach so you can soak in the sun, sea and surf anytime you want. ​ Once you get here, you won't want to miss any of the incredible attractions on the map below. facts.summaryText: --> Sustainablity | Texas Sandfest top of page At SandFest Daily Schedule Things To Do Live Music Kids Corner Sustainablity Parking & Shuttles Pets Accessibility Notices About Our Mission FAQ's Magazine Guest Survey Meet the Board Get Involved Vendors Sponsorship Volunteer Sculptors Winners Master Solo Sculptors Master Duo Sculptors Semi-Pro Sculptors Non-Competing Sculptors Advanced Amateurs Amateur Competition Media Room Press Release Tickets Golf Cart Raffle General Admission VIP Wristbands Contact Us More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Sustainability at Texas SandFest Texas SandFest is more than just a good time! Texas SandFest prides itself on keeping the beaches in Port Aransas better than they found them and protecting our environment. With initiatives toward recycling happening throughout the event, it's important to remember that sustainability is one of the number one goals for SandFest. Texas Sandfest is proud to partner with Keep Port Aransas Beautiful, Pitch in Port Aransas, the Mills Family, Gulf Coast Growth Ventures , and CORE Projects Energy on our ongoing sustainability initiative. ​ ​ ​ Keep Port Aransas Beautiful (KPAB) Keep Port Aransas Beautiful is a proud affiliate of Keep Texas Beautiful and Keep America Beautiful. The organization has been designated a Gold Star Affiliate of Keep Texas Beautiful. LEARN MORE Keep Port Aransas Beautiful ​ The initial attraction to Port Aransas, for both locals and visitors, is the natural beauty of the area. The sandy coastal dunes, the crashing waves, the wildlife of both land and sea-it's difficult to imagine anything better. facts.title: Accessibility | Texas Sandfest
headings: Accessible parking is available for vehicles displaying a current, valid state-issued accessible placard or license plate.
Location: On the beach inside the bollards at Marker 12.5, near the North entrance of SandFest.
Details: Spaces are limited and available on a first-come, first-served basis. No spots may be held or reserved.
For best access, arrive early, as availability can fill quickly during peak times.
Accessible portable toilets are available throughout the event grounds. They are located at the north entrance, near the music stage, and at the south entrance.
Personal Mobility Devices (Guest-Owned Wheelchairs and Motorized Scooters)

Motorized scooters and wheelchairs are permitted at SandFest. However, most standard models perform poorly in sand. We strongly recommend using a beach wheelchair (see below) for improved mobility on the beach. ​
A limited number of beach wheelchairs are available for free use to help guests navigate the sand. These are non-motorized and require someone in your group to push them.
Usage time: 1 hour and 45 minutes per reservation/use.
Weight capacity: 250 pounds (maximum; includes occupant and any items carried).
Reservation: Highly recommended to guarantee availability. Only one reservation per person is allowed for the entire event. Attempts to bypass the system may result in cancellation.
How to reserve: Use the Reserve Now link at the bottom of this page.
Without reservation: Check availability at the Guest Relations Tent (North gate entrance, near beach marker 12.5). No guarantees without a reservation.
Pick up at the Guest Relations Tent (North gate entrance, near beach marker 12.5).
Arrive at least 15 minutes before your reserved time. Late arrival will not extend your usage period.
$20 cash deposit (fully refunded upon on-time return with no damage).
A current driver's license from someone in your group (held as collateral and returned upon drop-off).
Completed and signed beach wheelchair usage agreement.
Texas SandFest provides beach wheelchairs similar to standard all-terrain models with large wheels for sand (example pictured below).
We are not responsible for injury, damage, or loss related to use. Users assume responsibility for safe operation.
Return the wheelchair to the guest relations tent at the north gate, on time and in good condition, to receive your deposit and license back.
Only service animals are permitted. Please click here for more information.
For additional information or questions, contact us at: info@texassandfest.org

facts.title: FAQ's | Texas Sandfest
headings: What is the Pet Policy for 2026?
Texas SandFest is now a Service Animals Only event. Only Service Animals, as defined by the ADA, are permitted within the festival grounds.
Are Emotional Support Animals (ESAs) or therapy pets allowed?
No. Per the ADA, the above do not qualify as Service Animals and will not be admitted.
What are the responsibilities of a Service Animal handler?

A Service Animal must be under the control of its handler. Under the ADA, Service Animals must be harnessed, leashed, or tethered, unless the individual’s disability prevents using these devices or these devices interfere with the Service Animal’s safe, effective performance of tasks. In that case, the individual must maintain control of the animal through voice, signal, or other effective controls.
The animal must be housebroken and well-behaved.
The handler is fully responsible for the animal’s care, cleanup, and any damages caused.
Can I still bring my pet to the beach outside the festival?
Yes. This policy applies only to the Texas SandFest event grounds.
Where can I find more information on ADA requirements?
For official federal guidance, visit the ADA website.
Can I bring a wagon to the event?
Small children may be pulled in a wagon.
Are there any nearby hotels or accommodations?
Yes, there are many great options in Port Aransas. Plan your stay via the Port Aransas Tourism Bureau & Chamber of Commerce website.
Yes, folding chairs are permitted, provided they do not block pedestrian traffic.
Can I bring a beach umbrella or other shade structure?
How do I become a volunteer?
Please follow this link to sign up and learn more about volunteering.
Yes, most take place at the South end of the grounds in Kids' Corner , including Lesson Mountain, face painting, fun games, and more! ​
Do you have a list of local restaurants?
Yes! See the full list on the Port Aransas Tourism Bureau & Chamber of Commerce website.
How does the golf cart raffle work?
Are photos or videos taken at Texas SandFest? What about using my image?
Yes. Texas SandFest (and authorized partners) may photograph, film, video, audio-record, or otherwise record attendees and anyone else throughout the event area.

By entering the premises, from 3 weeks before the event through the day after it closes (including setup, the festival, and teardown, you grant Texas SandFest, its affiliates, and licensees irrevocable permission to capture, use, publish, stream, display, reproduce, and distribute your image, likeness, voice, or appearance (with or without your name) without compensation. This applies to any media now known or developed in the future, worldwide, and in perpetuity—for promotional, commercial, news, or other purposes.
Your presence constitutes consent; no individual releases are needed. By entering, you also waive claims related to compensation, approval rights, privacy, publicity, or similar issues.
Even if you are just watching the sculptors build or walking through the area, you are included. ​
For the full details, see our Crowd Release / Notice of Filming and Photography. ​
On the beach between Markers 13 and 17. North gate entrance: Marker 13. South gate entrance: Marker 17.
Can we park our RV at Texas SandFest?
Yes, outside the bollards south of Marker 19 (perpendicular parking only, max 3 nights). See City of Port Aransas RV parking regulations .
No, inside the bollards, and no RVs, trailers, or oversized vehicles in SandFest parking lots. Texas SandFest assumes no liability for stuck vehicles, damage, or loss of property.
Can I park my trailer, RV, or other oversized vehicle inside the general Texas SandFest beach parking lots?
No, none are permitted in public SandFest beach parking lots. Park outside the bollards, per City rules (see above), or in the Gracies Way parking lot.
Where can I park my trailer, RV, or other oversized vehicle?
This parking lot is a field; park at your own risk.
Can I park my RV on the beach outside Texas SandFest?

Generally, yes on the west side of the bollards, north of Ave G or south of Marker 19, which is just south of Access Road 1A. No RV/trailer parking between Ave G and Marker 19 during the week leading up to the event and during the event. Obey all city ordinances and signage.
Will traffic be one-way on the beach?
Yes, typically southbound from ~9:00 AM to ~7:00 PM (Friday, Saturday & Sunday) between Ave G and Access Road 1A. Both directions north of Ave G and south of Access Road 1A.
SandFest Beach Access & Parking The Best Route: 📍 Avenue G is your best option for accessing beach parking via vehicle or golf cart.
Will I need a beach parking permit if I park in the Texas SandFest parking lots on the beach?
Yes, it is required for any beach parking. More info here .
Is there a cost to park?
No, Texas SandFest does not charge for parking; the City requires a $12 parking permit (calendar year) for all beach parking. All three remote lots are free and do not require a parking permit.
Where are the remote parking lots located?
Gracie's Way :  just off Access Road 1A (grass field)
Roberts Point Park: 301 J.C. Barr Blvd.
Port Aransas Community Park: 700 Clark Parkway
Is overnight parking allowed in the parking lots?
No overnight parking is permitted in any Texas SandFest lots, including Gracie's Way.
All bus parking (tour, school, charter) is at the remote lot at Gracie's Way   (grass field)
Will there be a golf cart shuttle to take me to and from my car?

Yes and no. Yes, the shuttles will take you to the vicinity (generally ~1000 feet from where you parked), but not to your car. This applies to the main lot (inside bollards, ~Marker 12 to Ave G) and Gracie's Way .  Priority: disabilities, injuries, elderly.
What hours do the golf cart shuttles operate?
9:00 AM until 8:00 PM sharp each event day.
Can we drive our golf carts inside the event?
No, only official Texas SandFest golf carts allowed.
Is there a lot of walking involved?

Advanced Amateurs On their way to becoming Pros! Being a Semi-Pro or Master Sculptor doesn't happen overnight. These Amateurs have spent years perfecting their skills and are well on their way to becoming Pro's. Come cheer them on! David Brandon - Texas/ Portugal This is David Brandon’s 8th Sandfest. He is from Austin Texas but, since retiring from a career in IT in 2025, he moved to Ferragudo, Portugal; where there are over 100 sandy beaches within a 30-minute drive. David has sculpted sand around the world and now, since retiring, can sculpt frequently and teach sand castle building lessons. His favorite sculpting beaches are Port Aransas Texas, Praia da Marina de Portimão Portugal, Praia Angrinha de Ferragudo Portugal, and al. Janet Moore Schrader - Michigan At the age of 36, Janet was diagnosed with Stage 4 non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma. With an eye patch and hair loss, she resembled a pirate. To cope with the effects of cancer and its treatment, she began walking along her favorite spot: the shores of Lake Michigan. One day, Janet built a small sandcastle. Though it wasn't particularly impressive, she was captivated by the experience. Finding the book Sand Castles Step-by-Step at her local library changed her life.

Bollard Buddies Port Aransas has been yarn-bombed! When you drive down the Port Aransas beach, you might find a welcome surprise made of yarn. The little bundles of joy that sit atop the bollards on the beach are called "Bollard Buddies". These fun friends bring smiles to everyone who sees them and are just another thing that makes Port Aransas such a special place. The Full Story ​ Diana Vondra, the founder of Bollard Buddies, answered questions many people have been dying to know about these fun little beach friends. "My husband and I were traveling in France and we came upon a street decorated in yarn. The trees, bike racks, railings and even doorknobs were yarn bombed. I immediately thought of Port Aransas. I had a friend Lorranie Care measure the bollards and began making sweaters for them.", said Diana. This is the 8th year the bollard buddies are being displayed in Port Aransas. The first year Diana put the buddies up, she started with 82. Now, there are over 400 on the Port Aransas beach during the winter months. Wondering how these cute little crafts are made? The History of SandFest Texas SandFest began in 1997 as a small local competition for sand sculptors to showcase their abilities and talent. It was organized by the Port Aransas Art Center, formerly the Art Center for the Islands and led by Sharon Schafer. The event is held every year in Port Aransas over a weekend in April. Port Aransas Community Theatre (PACT) was formed and all Texas SandFest business was conducted under the umbrella of the theatre. The event would be considered a fundraiser for the theater and PACT would receive the profits from the sand sculpting event. ​ The event has successfully brought thousands of people to visit the island. This has provided extra business opportunities for restaurants, shops and local merchants. By 2006, a request was made to release the festival to stand alone as a nonprofit group. The new theatre was built by PACT after several years of fundraising. The community was willing to donate more to the theatre group. People were more responsive to an event that could spread profits to other non-profit groups in the community. Texas SandFest became a nonprofit organization when it filed and received its 501(c)3 nonprofit status. It began work under a selected Board of Directors. 200

How to Get to SandFest Many people wonder what's the best way to get to SandFest? You can find the best places to park here . But getting to Port A has options to consider. ​ First, it is important to understand that the beach road is often restricted to one-way traffic heading southbound from Avenue G to Access Road 1A. This starts at about 8:00 AM and remains this way until about 8:00 PM on each day of the event. ​ So, whether you are coming in from the south via 361 North, or coming across the ferry landing to Port Aransas, you should definitely map out where you wish to access the festival. During peak times, you can expect the main beach access points to be backed up with slow moving traffic. ​ There are many Shuttle pickup points around the town, and there are many parking lots to consider. The most popular parking lots are the North Gate Parking lot around beach marker 9 primarily accessible from Avenue G, and the South Gate parking lot near Mile Marker 19. ​ Pack a little patience as you enter and leave the area and take some time to enjoy the salty breeze and sunshine. We are honored to report that $274,419 of those funds were awarded as scholarships, with the remaining $189,750 distributed among our community-driven initiatives and local charitable organizations. ​ This is part of our growing legacy of giving: since 2012, Texas SandFest's total contributions have surpassed $3.3 Million . Become a Volunteer! We love our Volunteers and are so greatful for your interest in becoming a Volunteer for Texas SandFest. Our success would not be possible without the time and support of our volunteers. General Volunteer Information ​ ​ ​ The minimum age to volunteer for all positions is 14. This event is outside, and almost all jobs will be in the sun and elements, including but not limited to heat, wind, and rain. The majority of jobs require standing and/ or walking for most of the shift. Most shifts are approximately 5.5 hours. Some jobs have a minimum age requirement and/or require a valid driver's license. Anyone driving equipment and/or a golf cart must be 25 years old​ Need assistance? About Texas SandFest Texas SandFest is a 501c(3) nonprofit that operates under a Board of Directors. What began as a small, local competition in 1997 has grown into an internationally recognized three-day family event that draws renowned sand sculptors and tens of thousands of visitors from around the world each year to Port Aransas. ​ You'll want to plan a trip and stay a while for this flagship festival. Attendees stroll through the festival enjoying mind-blowing sand sculptors, bopping their heads to live music and getting a taste of the coast from local food vendors. Adults can stop for a drink at the beer garden before letting their kids learn at "Lesson Mountain" or to hydrate before hitting up vendors for arts and crafts, apparel, jewelry, furniture, souvenirs and more. ​ The Master's Gallery is where the competition sculptures are created. Magnificent works of art, created over three days of literal tons of sand, can be found within this area. Sculptors work as solos or as teams of two and begin planning their sculpture weeks, even months, before the event to determine use of forms and what tools are needed. Master Solo Sculptors The grains of sand meet up with a single artist to create a mastery in sand sculptors. you will stand in awe of what these masters of the art of sandcastle sculpting will create right in front of your eyes. They know what they want, they know how to do it, and they build a masterpiece on the shore. Damon Langlois Damon Langlois is a Canadian sand sculptor and designer known for his competitive work at major international sand sculpture events, including Texas SandFest. He is a five-time World Championship of Sand Sculpting winner and has competed at the master level for decades. Langlois gained widespread attention in the competitive sand sculpture world with his 2019 Texas SandFest entry Liberty Crumbling, The piece, depicting a facepalming Abraham Lincoln, has continued to be one of the most shared sand sculptures from the event and remains among his best-known competitive works. In addition to his competition achievements, Langlois was the creative director and engineer that set the Guinness World Record for the tallest sand castle in 2015. Newsroom Media Contacts: Please email inquiries to: media@texassandfest.org Texas SandFest Announces Service Animals Only Policy PORT ARANSAS, TX – February 2, 2026 – Texas SandFest is adopting a Service Animals Only policy. “Our goal is to keep Texas SandFest a fun and enjoyable destination for every guest and their families,” said Tim Parke, President. “We know how much guests cherish bringing their pets, and this decision wasn’t made lightly. Texas SandFest is fully committed to ADA accessibility. We’re deeply grateful to our pet-loving community for understanding and supporting this step.” Under the ADA, only dogs (any breed) or miniature horses that perform disability-related tasks qualify as Service Animals. Emotional support animals, therapy animals, comfort animals, pets, and all other animals do not qualify and will not be permitted. Handlers must keep Service Animals under control, ensure they are housebroken and well-behaved, and handle all care/cleanup. For official ADA guidance ADA.gov Texas SandFest 2026 takes place April 17–19 on the beach in Port Aransas. For details, tickets, or planning, visit www.TexasSandFest.org . for the full Pet Policy. Non-Competing Master Sculptors It's not all about winning, it's about having fun! You don't have to compete in Texas SandFest to enjoy all the fun of creating sand sculptures. These artists have sculpted under the Port A sun just for fun. Learn more about these sculptors below. Mitch Guerrero - SWAT Team Mitch is going on his 10th year as a sand sculptor and due to his full time work as a sand sculptor he has decided to become a professional artist and pursue art as a career. This will be his second season as a lead member of the S.W.A.T team at the Texas SandFest! In October of 2023, Mitch decided to walk away from a 23-year desk job as a senior website designer to pursue his love for this art form and his love for art in general. When he is not competing, Mitch likes to teach sandcastle lessons to tourists on Siesta Key Beach in Sarasota, Florida. As a 4th generation artist, he also loves to draw, paint and use clay to create sculptures. Marc Mangia - SWAT Team I grew up and currently live in Columbus Ohio. Ferry Get Ferried Around There are two main ways to get into Port Aransas - from the South side of the island via State Highway 361 or across the ferry from the North. Of the two, the second is the more exciting by a landslide. When you board the ferry on the Aransas Pass side and cross that strip of water into Port Aransas, your easiest mode of Transportaion also becomes the portal to your vacation destination. Enjoy the short ten-minute ride from your car or walk aboard to watch the dolphins play in the wake and see huge ships pass through the channel. ​ ​ For more detailed information, including real-time ferry waits, ferry rules, and more, see the Texas Department of Transportation . For ferry camera viewing, see webcams . Operation Times The Port Aransas ferry system, one of only two total in Texas, operates 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, weather permitting. Anywhere from two to six ferry boats run in a day, depending on traffic and season. ​ The ferry will be closed in event of a severe storm or hurricane making landfall near Port A, as increased water heights mean the ferries cannon be safely secured at the loading and unloading ramps. ​ ​ Boarding Traffic coming off the ferry from Aransas Pass can head straight onto

Semi-Pro Sculptors These artists' journey has not just begun, but their skills continue to strengthen with each carving of a sandcastle they create and each competition in which they spend time building out an artistry of sand. You won't want to miss watching these semi-pro artists transform into excellence right before your eyes with unbelievable talent create sand sculptures worth remembering. Darrell O'Connor I live in Wilmington Delaware, but for the last 40 years I’ve spent summer weekends building sandcastles at Dewey Beach. My journey into sand sculpting began in the mid 1980’s when I entered my first contest at Rehoboth Beach. While I didn’t place in that first competition, I continued practicing and refining my skills, mostly through trial and error, with the occasional “how-to” book for guidance. The practice paid off and I went on to win the Rehoboth contest nine times. Everything changed in 2014 when I entered my first advanced amateur contest in Wildwood New Jersey. There I was fortunate enough to meet a master sand sculptor, and our friendship became a turning point in the quality of my work. facts.title: Pets | Texas Sandfest
headings: We have made the tough call to update our Pet Policy. Effective for 2026 and moving forward, Texas SandFest will be a Service Animals Only event.
Only Service Animals, as defined by the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), are permitted inside the event area.
We truly understand this change may feel disappointing for many of you who look forward to sharing the sand with your dogs. We get it, and we're grateful for your understanding.
What qualifies as a Service Animal? Under the ADA, a Service Animal is a dog (any breed) or miniature horse that performs work or tasks directly related to a person’s disability.
What does NOT qualify? Emotional support animals, therapy animals, comfort animals, companion animals, and pets do not qualify as Service Animals under the ADA and will not be admitted.

The Service Animal must be under the control of its handler. Under the ADA, Service Animals must be harnessed, leashed, or tethered, unless the individual’s disability prevents using these devices or these devices interfere with the Service Animal’s safe, effective performance of tasks. In that case, the individual must maintain control of the animal through voice, signal, or other effective controls.
The animal must be housebroken and well-behaved.
The handler is fully responsible for the animal’s care, cleanup, and any damages caused.
Texas SandFest remains fully committed to ADA compliance and accessibility for all. For official ADA guidance, visit: ADA.gov
Note: While the festival grounds are now limited to service animals only, your pups are still welcome to enjoy the rest of the beach outside our gates.
Questions? We're here to help. Contact us at  info@texassandfest.org.

Things to do at Texas SandFest You've probably never dreamed that one day you'd be headed to the Port Aransas beach to see people carve majestic works of art right in front of your eyes out of small grains of sand. You can take a crack at learning how to build your own sandcastles, enjoy many kid-friendly activities and dance your worries away in the sand as the line-up of great musical acts await. ​ Plan your day and weekend to make the most of your time in Port Aransas, the True Texas Coast! ​ ​ Live Music Texas SandFest is your chance to see live music performed right on the sand! Master Duo Sculptors It takes two to...sand sculpt? Some say it takes two to make a thing go right...and these artists totally believe it and practice it! These teams of two join forces as a duo to be reckoned with as they tackle the sand together to make their artistic vision come ot life masterfully in the sand. Chris Guinto & Melineigh Beauregard "If you ain't first, you're last." This has been the family mantra sine granddaddy Guinto invented fire and Chris continues to carry that torch to this day. Evident in his sculpturesin which millions have made pilgrimages to wittness, words like "Supernatural", "Divine Providence" and "Number one on my bucket list" are often used to describe his sculptures. In a nutshell, as Chuck Norris once said: "Sculpting? I leave that to Chris Guinto". That was true until he met his nemesis, Melineigh Beauregard. It was at that point he truly understood he might not stand at the top of the food chain anymore. Opposites attract, and like fire and water, they've competed for the "King of the Hill" title, pushing each other to the extreme limits of their abilities.
